category_title: Preschoolers with frequent inappropriate social behaviors (yelling, taking toys, crying)
minimal_context: Gregory is participating in free play time with his peers in the preschool classroom.
dialogue:
TUTOR: It is time for free play. Go pick a center and play with the other children.

STUDENT: (Walks over to a peer) I want those! (Takes the other child's toys)

TUTOR: Let's try to play together.

STUDENT: (Hides the toys behind his back) No!

TUTOR: Remember to use your words.

STUDENT: (Yells at the other child and starts crying) They are mine!

options.A: Use a watch timer to check in right before each routine change for 30 seconds; circle the thumbs-up or thumbs-down picture for the preceding activity and give verbal feedback about specific behaviors.
options.B: Use a 'request for clarification' during the free play activity, asking Gregory for explanations and elaborations regarding the toys and his interactions with peers.
options.C: Follow Gregory's lead and keep a physical distance from him and the other children during the free play activity in the preschool classroom.
options.D: Teach a replacement behavior for when Gregory wants a peer's toys, practicing this new skill during the free play activity in the preschool classroom.
